creator: chris.dukes.aix@gmail.com
text:
On Thu, Jun 27, 2013 at 06:41:01PM +0300, Volodymyr Kostyrko wrote:

[SNEEP]

I think I answered most of those questions when I did a followup
back on April 24th.

CSTD is being set, it is supposed to be set.

If it were only being passed to 'clang' it would be fine,
but because it's being passed to 'clang++' it's going to hell.

It's getting passed to clang++ because the configure.ac is doing
two instances of 
$PKGCONFIG --cflags <LUACRUFT>
instead of 
$PKGCONFIG --cflags-only-I <LUACRUFT>

Patching the configure.ac is easy.
Regenerating configure is something I've not done recently enough to trust
what I did.

And the WITH_CLANG_IS_CC flag seems to set CC=clang, but not
CXX=clang++

Anyhow setting CC, CXX, and CPP individually is as directed by
https://wiki.freebsd.org/BuildingFreeBSDWithClang

I'd love to run with WITH_CLANG_IS_CC, instead, but it doesn't
seem to switch everything.

If it does, it's if I do a custom built base OS installation.
The only reason I'm using 9-stable for my build machine is because
poudriere exercises a ZFS bug that was fixed in 9-stable.

-- 
Chris Dukes.  
This message is not confidential.  Due to the nature of how email is 
sent, it's only slightly more private than a postcard.  Use GPG if you 
want correspondence to be confidential and private.
